skill.name: data-cleaning-pipeline
skill.description: Automates planning and guidance for data cleaning workflows, including deduplication, missing value handling, standardization, and anomaly detection.
skill.summary: Create repeatable data cleaning plans for messy datasets.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:4 is a false positive from metadata text, not executable cryptographic code. The high entropy finding at SKILL.md:1 is also a false positive caused by short frontmatter and non-ASCII content; no obfuscated payload, prompt injection, scripts, network access, filesystem access, or external command usage was found.

content.user_title: Clean Messy Data With a Pipeline
content.value_statement: Messy datasets slow analysis and create unreliable results. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan cleaning steps for deduplication, missing values, formatting, and anomaly checks.
actual_capabilities: Guides deduplication planning for tabular datasets.
Suggests approaches for missing value completion and review.
Helps standardize dates, categories, and common field formats.
Outlines anomaly detection checks for suspicious records.
Produces human-readable cleaning workflows for review.
limitations: Does not include executable cleaning scripts in the skill package.
Requires the user to provide dataset context and quality goals.
Critical data changes still need human review before use.
Does not verify results against external data sources.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sales Data
use_cases.description: Create a cleaning plan for duplicated customers, inconsistent dates, and missing revenue fields.

use_cases.title: Review Survey Responses
use_cases.description: Plan checks for blank answers, inconsistent labels, and outlier response patterns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Reporting Inputs
use_cases.description: Define repeatable cleanup steps before loading weekly files into dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Cleaning Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a messy CSV with duplicate rows, blank fields, and inconsistent date formats. Create a simple cleaning plan.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Data Quality Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these column names and sample issues. Rank the cleaning tasks by risk and explain what to fix first.

prompt_templates.title: Design Validation Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Design validation checks for customer, order, and payment fields before this dataset enters an analytics workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Repeatable Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable data cleaning workflow with checkpoints, review steps, and acceptance criteria for weekly imports.

output_examples.input: Customer export has duplicate emails, blank phone numbers, and mixed country names.
output_examples.output: The output would list cleanup priorities, matching rules for duplicates, completion guidance for missing fields, and standard country naming checks.

output_examples.input: Survey data includes free-text labels, skipped answers, and impossible age values.
output_examples.output: The output would propose normalization rules, missing response categories, age range validation, and a review queue for unusual records.

output_examples.input: Weekly sales files arrive with changing column names and inconsistent currency formats.
output_examples.output: The output would define column mapping checks, currency standardization steps, and approval criteria before dashboard loading.

best_practices: Share sample columns, known issues, and the intended analysis before asking for a plan.
Keep a copy of the original dataset before applying any cleaning changes.
Review high-impact changes, such as deduplication and imputation, with a domain owner.
anti_patterns: Do not apply automatic fixes to regulated or financial data without review.
Do not remove outliers until you understand whether they represent real events.
Do not mix exploratory cleanup with final production rules in the same workflow.

faq.question: Does this skill clean files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The package provides AI guidance for cleaning workflows, not executable file processing code.

faq.question: Which data types work best?
faq.answer: It works best for tabular datasets such as CSV exports, spreadsheets, and analytics input files.

faq.question: Can it handle missing values?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest ways to flag, fill, or exclude missing values based on context.

faq.question: Can it detect anomalies?
faq.answer: It can outline anomaly checks and review criteria, but users must validate results with real data.

faq.question: Is this useful for ETL work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help define cleaning steps before loading data into reporting or analytics systems.

faq.question: Does it require Python?
faq.answer: No. Python is listed as a related tag, but the skill itself is prompt-based guidance.
